content.problemStatement:
Chef has started learning to solve dynamic programming problems recently. He has practiced on a lot of problems already, so he wants to try his hands on the hardest problems in this domain.

Chef only likes the interesting part of solving dynamic programming problems — specifically, he just likes to divide a problem into beautiful subproblems which consist of solving recurrences; he finds actually solving these recurrences quite boring. Since you are Chef's assistant, your job is solving this boring part for Chef.

Let's define a recurrent sequence {dp(k)}k=1∞\left\lbrace{dp(k)}\right\rbrace_{k=1}^\infty{dp(k)}k=1∞​ with parametres AAA, BBB and KKK as follows:


dp(1)=Kdp(1) = Kdp(1)=K
for n>1n \gt 1n>1, dp(n)=A⋅dp(n−1)+B⋅∑i=1n−1dp(i)⋅dp(n−i)dp(n) = A \cdot dp(n-1) + B\cdot \sum\limits_{i=1}^{n-1} dp(i) \cdot dp(n-i)dp(n)=A⋅dp(n−1)+B⋅i=1∑n−1​dp(i)⋅dp(n−i)
Chef would like you to find dp(N)dp(N)dp(N). Since this number can be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
content.inputFormat: The first and only line of the input contains four space-separated integers N, K, A, and B.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer — the value of dp(N) modulo 10^9+7.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first four terms of the sequence are: dp(1) = 1, dp(2) = 3, dp(3) = 12, dp(4) = 57
The first five terms of the sequence are: dp(1) = 2, dp(2) = 10, dp(3) = 82, dp(4) = 730, dp(5) = 3059

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we will use dynamic programming to compute the value of dp(N) modulo 10^9+7. We will start by initializing an array dp of size N+1, where dp[i] will store the value of dp(i) modulo 10^9+7. We will then fill up the dp array using the given recurrence relation. Finally, we will return the value of dp[N].
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) — because we are using a nested loop to fill up the dp array
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — because we are using an array of size N+1 to store the dp values

code.Python3:
MOD = 1000000007
maxN = 10000001
dp = [0] * maxN
def solve(N, K, A, B):
    dp[1] = K
    for i in range(2, N + 1):
        sum = 0
        for j in range(1, i):
            sum = (sum + dp[j] * dp[i - j]) % MOD
        dp[i] = (A * dp[i - 1] + B * sum) % MOD
    return dp[N]
N, K, A, B = map(int, input().split())
print(solve(N, K, A, B))
